title: leptaspis banksii
intro: Leptaspis banksii, also known as the Banks' grass-lily or Banks' lilyturf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. This plant is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Leptaspis banksii include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Leptaspis banksii can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it has also been traditionally used as a poultice or applied directly to the skin for its medicinal benefits.
